metadata.title: Why the One-Sentence Problem Statement Is the Most Expensive Thing You Write

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Merhaba, Liam — in for Bear. Why did it take fourteen minutes and two refusals to produce one valid sentence? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Why did it take fourteen minutes and two refusals to produce one valid sentence??
props.segment: Why the One-Sentence Problem Statement Is

beats.narration_text: Seth types: an agent that audits Unity scripts and refactors them and writes the missing tests and generates a CLAUDE.md. Refused. Two ands — four projects dressed as one. He stares at the screen. He thinks about which one he actually wants. Fourteen minutes later he types twenty-eight words. One sentence. No code. That sentence is the most valuable thing he writes this semester.
Writing one sentence should be fast. Seth had been thinking about this project for three days. Why did it take fourteen minutes and two refusals to produce one valid sentence?

The one-sentence constraint is not about writing style. It is an elevator test for whether you know what you're building. A sentence with two ands is two systems dressed as one — different users, different inputs, different done-conditions. The refusal forces the decision that the list of features was deferring. Seth had thought he was building one thing. He was postponing a choice. The fourteen minutes is not wasted time. It is the essential work.

on_screen: One system. One user. One done-condition.
beats.narration_text: The sentence that passes names one system, one user, one done-condition — and contains no ands. The sentence that passes is the moment you take ownership of the project's conceptual integrity. Everything Claude produces from there gets measured against that sentence. The sentence costs fourteen minutes. Skipping it costs the build.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why the One-Sentence Problem Statement Is the Most Expensive Thing You] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
